data.title: Backfill: process historical conversation archive
data.description:
## Parent: #1

## Depends on: #3

## Summary

Run the extraction pipeline against 1.5 years of historical conversations. This is the bulk processing job that turns dead JSONL files into searchable, structured knowledge.

## Scale

* \~900k lines of conversation history
* Unknown number of conversations (need to scan)
* Only viable with local LLM (ollama) — API costs would be prohibitive

## CLI

```bash
claude-memory backfill --types all           # everything
claude-memory backfill --types decisions     # just decisions
claude-memory backfill --after 2025-01-01    # date range
claude-memory backfill --project <name>      # specific project
claude-memory backfill --dry-run             # count what would be processed
```

## Implementation

1. Scan archive for conversations without extraction output files
2. Queue jobs via existing queue system
3. Process in batches (queue already handles this)
4. Track progress — resumable if interrupted

## Considerations

* This will generate thousands of queue jobs — queue needs to handle gracefully
* Progress reporting: `backfill --status` to show completion %
* Rate limiting for ollama (don't saturate local GPU for hours)
* Priority: newer conversations first, oldest last
* Historical conversations may reference obsolete projects/patterns — extraction should still capture them
